channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Germany accuses Musk of trying to sway election
item.description: The German government on Monday accused Elon Musk of trying to influence its February election after the world’s wealthiest person penned an op-ed this weekend backing the far-right political party Alternative for Germany (AfD).
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Former US lieutenant general warns Musk poses national security risks
item.description: A former U.S. lieutenant general warned Sunday that Elon Musk, the tech billionaire who has become intricately involved in President-elect Trump’s transition, may pose national security risks due to his ties to China and Russia. Retired Lt. Gen.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Atos database reportedly breached by hackers
item.description: Atos says it’s been warned of a potential ransomware attack, but there has been no concrete evidence so far.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Change management: Achieve your goals with the right change model
item.description: Change and transformation. Everyone talks about it and knows how necessary it is to adapt quickly to permanently changing conditions. And yet change is difficult.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: In 2024, artificial intelligence was all about putting AI tools to work
item.description: Just over two years after the debut of ChatGPT, the world is getting a little savvier about what artificial intelligence technology can do for us and what it’s not so great at.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien And Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Samsung Phones in 2025: Galaxy Z Fold 7, Flip 7, and More
item.description: Samsung phones in 2025: Expectations are high with the Galaxy Z Fold 7, Galaxy Z Flip 7, and possibly a tri-fold phone.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Hands-on with Adobe’s Acrobat AI Assistant: AI for your PDFs
item.description: We may be on the brink of 2025, but PDFs are still unavoidable in the professional world. No matter what industry you work in, you’re bound to whittle away precious moments wading through reports, white papers, and other dense documents in that clunky-feeling form.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Great Open Source Debate: Does Meta's Refusal to Cede Control Matter?
item.description: The Open Source Initiative (OSI) chief Stefano Maffulli claims Meta is 'bullying' the industry on open source, but does it really matter?
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 10 hot programming trends
item.description: Tides ebb and flow. Pendulums swing. Ideas come and go, especially in programming, where the technologies and techniques for creating software continuously evolve. Some programming languages, libraries, platforms, and tools become hot and irresistible.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New York state government to monitor its use of AI under a new law
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York state government agencies will have to conduct reviews and publish reports that detail how they're using artificial intelligence software, under a new law signed by Gov. Kathy Hochul.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Critical warning from Microsoft: .NET install domains changing
item.description: Users who expected to be able to use the resources of the Azure content delivery network (CDN) from Edgio until November 2025, as previously announced, had a not-so-festive “gift” this week – the CDN is now shutting down on Jan. 15, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A 9th telecoms firm has been hit by a massive Chinese espionage campaign, the White House says
item.description: A top White House official says a ninth U.S. telecoms firm has been confirmed to have been hacked as part of a sprawling Chinese espionage campaign that gave officials in Beijing access to private texts and phone conversations of an unknown number of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lincoln returns to port after proving out ‘game changing’ connectivity
item.description: On its latest deployment, the Nimitz-class carrier went from slow military satellite uplinks to modern capabilities that move terabytes of data each day. The post Lincoln returns to port after proving out ‘game changing’ connectivity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump says Bill Gates asked to visit Mar-a-Lago
item.description: President-elect Trump said Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates asked to visit his Florida resort Mar-a-Lago on Friday night. "Where are you? When are you coming to the 'Center of the Universe,' Mar-a-Lago. Bill Gates asked to come, tonight," Trump wrote in a Friday morning Truth Social post.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Mexico tests cellphone app allowing migrants to send alert if they are about to be detained in US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico is developing a cellphone app that will allow migrants to warn relatives and local consulates if they think they are about to be detained by the U.S. immigration department, a senior official said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sign PDFs easily in Preview on Apple devices
item.description: With electronic signatures becoming more common, Kurt the CyberGuy explains how to produce your digital John Hancock for online documents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cyberattack hits Japan Airlines, delaying flights for holiday travelers
item.description: Japan Airlines said it was hit by a cyberattack Thursday, causing delays to more than 20 domestic flights but the carrier said it was able to stop the onslaught and restore its systems hours later. There was no impact on flight safety, it said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Homeland Security launches internal AI chatbot for 19,000 staff
item.description: The tool, called DHSChat, will be available to approximately 19,000 employees at DHS headquarters and select pilot users across ten operating agencies.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation
